content: How does the Bible describe Job?
explanation: Job 1:1 - Job was blameless and upright, one who feared God and shunned evil.
options: Blameless, upright, God-fearing, and shunning evil

content: Why did Satan accuse Job of fearing God?
explanation: Job 1:9-11 - Satan accused Job of believing only for personal gain. God allowed testing to prove Job's genuine faith.
options: Because God protected and blessed him, not from a sincere heart

content: What did Job's wife tell him to do, and how did Job respond?
explanation: Job 2:9-10 - Job refused to abandon his faith even when his closest companion urged him to give it up.
options: Wife said: curse God and die. Job: shall we receive good and not accept trouble?

content: What did Job declare even while suffering?
explanation: Job 13:15 - Unconditional faith: trusting God even in the worst circumstances.
options: Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him

content: Job declared: "I know that my ___ lives"
explanation: Job 19:25 - In the midst of suffering, Job declared his faith in the living Redeemer and the resurrection.

content: From where did God answer Job?
explanation: Job 38:1 - God spoke from the whirlwind, asking Job many questions about creation to reveal His infinite wisdom.

content: After God spoke, how did Job respond?
explanation: Job 40:4-5 - Job recognized his smallness before God's greatness and fell silent.
options: Laid his hand over his mouth and spoke no more

content: Job said: "I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear, but now ___"
explanation: Job 42:5 - From hearsay to personal experience. Suffering led Job to a deeper knowledge of God.

content: In the end, how much did God give Job compared to before?
explanation: Job 42:10 - God restored double. The Lord is compassionate to those who patiently endure (James 5:11).

content: What does Job 28:28 conclude true wisdom is?
explanation: Job 28:28 - Similar to Proverbs 1:7 and Ecclesiastes 12:13. Wisdom begins and ends with the fear of the Lord.
options: The fear of the Lord and turning away from evil

content: Eliphaz said: "Blessed is the one whom God ___"
explanation: Job 5:17 - God's correction is a sign of love, not abandonment (Hebrews 12:6).
options: Corrects; do not despise the chastening of the Almighty

content: Why did God rebuke Job's three friends?
explanation: Job 42:7 - Though Job lamented, he remained honest with God. The three friends had tidy doctrine but were wrong about God's character.
options: They did not speak correctly about God as Job did

content: After losing everything, what famous statement did Job make?
explanation: Job 1:21 — 'Naked I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return. The LORD gave, and the LORD has taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.'
options: The LORD gave and the LORD has taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD
Why did God do this to me?

content: In Job 3:1, what did Job open his mouth to curse?
explanation: Job 3:1 — 'After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.'

content: According to Eliphaz in Job 4:7, does the innocent ever perish?
explanation: Job 4:7 — 'Remember: who that was innocent ever perished? Or where were the upright cut off?'
options: Eliphaz claims the innocent never perish

content: How does Job 14:1 describe a man born of woman?
explanation: Job 14:1 — 'Man who is born of a woman is few of days and full of trouble.'
options: Few of days and full of trouble

content: What does Job 23:10 say will be the result when God has tested Job?
explanation: Job 23:10 — 'But he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold.'

content: Job 31:1 says he made a covenant with what?
explanation: Job 31:1 — 'I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze at a virgin?'

content: When God laid the foundations of the earth, according to Job 38:7, who sang and shouted for joy?
explanation: Job 38:7 — 'When the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy.'
options: The morning stars and sons of God

content: What giant creature does Job 41 describe that no one can catch with a fishhook?
explanation: Job 41:1 — 'Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook or press down his tongue with a cord?'
